mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly drives equity valuations by expanding corporate earnings expectations. Sustained positive GDPC1 growth compresses equity risk premiums and lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in positive GDPC1 growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates exceeding 5.0% signal restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates for future cash flows and tightens financial conditions, depressing equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in the unemployment rate and declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate labor market deterioration that precedes corporate revenue contractions. This joint signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in at least 40% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which supports corporate margins and drives positive short-term equity returns as investors price in improved operational efficiency.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO month-over-month changes do not yield statistically significant positive 5-day forward returns (t-stat < 1.96) over a 5-year backtest, or the signal's Sharpe ratio falls below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly housing starts serve as a leading indicator of consumer confidence and construction-related economic activity. Sustained levels above 1.4 million units signal robust aggregate demand, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: HOUST values above 1.4 million units fail to produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of historical instances, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals rapid labor market cooling that typically precedes earnings downgrades and equity drawdowns, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal incorrectly predicts negative returns in more than 65% of triggered instances over a 2-year walk-forward period, or the average 20-day forward return in triggered months is positive and statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: Falling real personal savings combined with rising consumer prices erode household purchasing power and signal demand destruction. This interaction predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as corporate revenues contract.
failure_condition: Instances of declining RSAFS alongside rising CPIAUCSL do not yield negative 60-day forward returns in at least 50% of out-of-sample cases, or the joint signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates paired with accelerating consumer price inflation indicate an aggressive policy response to overheating, which compresses equity multiples and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of historical instances, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a rolling 2-year regression.
